spec_match.rationale: Uses beforetoggle's cancellability to guard against close-when-dirty — the canonical use case the spec calls out.

interactive.rationale: Form input drives dirty state; closing the dialog while dirty triggers a confirmation pattern.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'are you sure?' guard via beforetoggle.preventDefault(); page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show the difference between cancel event and beforetoggle event ordering. Doesn't probe browser quirks where some close paths bypass the guard.

references.rationale: See also has no chromestatus.com link — sibling-only. Rule #3 regression.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, form controls follow design system.

openQuestions.title: Show cancel + beforetoggle event ordering
openQuestions.detail: When a modal dialog is closed via ESC, cancel fires first, then beforetoggle. Both are cancellable. Show event ordering in a live log so the visitor knows where to hook the guard.

summary: Canonical 'are you sure?' demo of the cancellable beforetoggle event. Missing the chromestatus reference link and never disambiguates cancel vs beforetoggle event ordering.
